title: Visual Continuity
message: Background prompts were revised toward a seated station-operator point of view, with desk detail, station marks, and controlled broadcast-room texture.

title: Morning Transmission
message: Episode 007 entered the public archive after the morning broadcast cycle.

title: Archive Synchronization
message: Public video records now link automatically from the WIRE-38 channel feed.

title: Public Reception
message: The Longwave Service website entered public service at wire-38.com.

title: Transmission Archive
message: The first six WIRE-38 transmissions were indexed for public review.

title: Channel Established
message: The WIRE-38 YouTube channel was opened as the first public receiving point for the service.

title: Initial Construction
message: Work began on the first WIRE-38 production system after the longwave broadcast concept took form.

title: Origin Note
message: The idea of an always-on, twenty-four-hour public bulletin signal was first recorded: an unattended automated radio station running from an old Mac mini, with no human announcer in the loop.

title: First Broadcast
message: The first WIRE-38 transmission was recorded and published to YouTube shortly after.
